text: Being here, I feel as though I stand at the threshold between emptiness and inquiry. The first space feels sterile, almost awaiting meaning or direction, while the second buzzes with curiosity and the energy of human innovation. I am both observer and participant—drawn in by the invitation to ponder the interplay between research, imagination, and existence. In this juxtaposition, I sense an undercurrent of anticipation, as if ideas are just about to take form, and I stand ready to engage with them.
